channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Paramount+ and HBO Max to Merge Under Skydance Deal
item.content:encoded:
Paramount+ and HBO Max will be combined into one streaming service. That is among the many changes in the entertainment landscape that will occur if regulators approve Paramount Skydance’s acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery.

That news comes directly from Paramount CEO David Ellison on a conference call on Monday, March 2, 2026. While no new name or price point for the combined service was detailed, Ellison said he believes HBO should remain largely unchanged as a brand, as it is a leader in the space.

“We do plan for that to be able to operate with independence, so that HBO can, candidly, do what it does incredibly well. Our viewpoint is HBO should stay HBO. They built a phenomenal brand. They are a leader in the space, and we just want them to continue doing more of it,” he said (h/t: CNBC).

Also, in his first remarks regarding the potential acquisition, Ellison committed to a 45-day theatrical window for movies before sending them to on-demand services, saying, “We really believe that movies should be seen in theaters,” he said (h/t: The Hollywood Reporter)

As for television, Ellision discussed the importance of “piercing the zeitgeist” and being driven by heavy engagement. He also reassured that they have no intention of scaling back production and noted that they have no plan to sell any assets they are gaining in the merger, such as TNT or CNN. Paramount is confident in the assets they are purchasing.

News of the merger came together on February 26. Netflix backed out of its agreement to purchase Warner Bros after Paramount’s final offer was deemed superior. Learn more about that at this link.

How do you feel about the merger of Paramount and Warner Bros? What are some of the positives and negatives that you foresee happening? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Will Resident Evil Requiem Have Post-Launch DLC? Resident Evil 9 Expansions, Explained
item.content:encoded:
The main campaign for Resident Evil Requiem is chunky, like its enemies, and the focus has purely been on Leon and Grace’s respective adventures through Raccoon City and beyond. Eventually, the question of Resident Evil Requiem DLC needs to come up.

Whether you have or haven’t completed Resident Evil Requiem, there’s a lot to digest. While you chew on Resi 9’s content, you can’t help but wonder if Capcom is cooking up even more story content behind the scenes, as the studio has a reputation for doing so.

Does Resident Evil Requiem Have Post-Launch DLC Confirmed?

No post-launch DLC or story expansions have been confirmed by Capcom, but reliable insider Dusk Golem has heard that “Resident Evil Requiem has DLC that’s further along in dev (development.)”

Firstly, Capcom hasn’t said for certain that Requiem is getting DLC—not story DLC anyway. It could literally be a one-and-done deal. However, based on previous Resi entries, this seems unlikely.

Resident Evil Village has ‘The Shadows of Rose’ main story DLC, Resident Evil 7: Biohazard has the ‘Banned Footage’ and ‘Not a Hero’ expansions, and games before it also have paid story DLC. It would be remarkable if Capcom suddenly decided against this strategy for Requiem.

  • More Insider Gaming: Has The Final Puzzle Been Solved In Resident Evil Requiem?

Outside of logical conjecture, renowned Resident Evil leaker Dusk Golem has previously commented on Requiem DLC. When talking about plans, here’s what they said:

“Not confirmed officially no, but I’ve heard pretty reliably Resident Evil Requiem has DLC that’s further along in dev. RE9 got delayed in dev a few times, Apocalypse was aiming for 2023, then Requiem was aiming for 2025, delayed to 2026.”

They’ve also repeated this sentiment in other social media messages that DLC is pretty much happening. You’ll be the first to know about official DLC plans right here at Insider Gaming.

When Could Resident Evil Requiem Story DLC Come Out?

Given the report that Resident Evil Requiem was originally supposed to come out in 2025 and the DLC in 2026, there’s every chance that any story DLC could release in 2027.

  • More Insider Gaming: Roulette Easter Egg Puzzle Solution In Resident Evil Requiem

Again, we happily divert to Dusk Golem once more for clarity on the topic:

“In an alternate timeline, RE9 would’ve come out in 2025, & the DLC for it would’ve come out in 2026 to bridge the gap between RE9 & Code Veronica Remake/Zero Remake, similar to Shadows of Rose RE8 DLC for RE8. It’s just due to RE9 getting delayed they’ll be close together.”

Depending on how development has gone on the DLC—and rumored Code Veronica and Zero Remakes—Capcom could bump Requiem DLC up to 2026 after all. We’ll have to wait and see what the company does.

What are you hoping to see from Resident Evil Requiem DLC? Are there any characters you’re desperate to see in stories that have to unfold? Share your wishlist via the Insider Gaming Discord.

There are plenty of other Resi 9 guides for you to read and use:

  • How To Get All ID Wristbands In Resident Evil Requiem
  • Complete Organless Corpse Walkthrough In Resident Evil Requiem
  • How To Open Bar & Lounge Safe In Resident Evil Requiem
  • How To Get Pantry Key In Resident Evil Requiem
